runTitle: Vercel broad production QA on v8.11.28 before landing profile label-in-name patch
coveredSurfaces: / landing render with guest profile state and version badge v8.11.28
/main redirect to /
/login OAuth/email/guest entry points and privacy link
/privacy render with back-link
404 route render
/api/health healthy response
/api/version production metadata response
/system-boot to /dashboard redirect
Dashboard counts 18/17/1/0 and system resources 34/46/42
Active alerts modal render and Escape close
Topology modal render with 15 nodes and 20 edges
Server detail modal 3-tab switch and Escape close
Log explorer modal render with filters and log list
Profile menu guest accessible name and no admin-only item
AI sidebar starter prompts, input, and right-panel feature menu
AI Chat summary response with analysis basis metadata
Fullscreen /dashboard/ai-assistant workspace and System Context render
Reporter empty-state to report generation and detail view
Analyst full-system analysis render for 18 servers
Feedback submission network 200
Playwright console capture 0 errors / 0 warnings
Chrome DevTools landing console 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 issues
skippedSurfaces: Prompt-injection blocked-input regression replay (MCP browser safety layer refused adversarial override text automation)
Direct Cloud Run admin /monitoring and /monitoring/traces checks
Direct X-AI response header proof capture

expertAssessments.rationale: Representative AI Chat, Reporter, Analyst, and feedback surfaces all executed successfully on production v8.11.28.
Browser console stayed clean, core AI endpoints returned 200s, and landing Lighthouse scores remained 100/100/100 aside from one accessibility rule finding on the profile button label-in-name check.

Chrome DevTools Lighthouse still flags label-content-name-mismatch on the landing profile button because the visible identity text does not align with the computed accessible name order expected by axe.
expertAssessments.nextAction: Reorder the landing/dashboard profile button accessible name so the visible identity text leads the computed name, then rerun production broad QA.

expertAssessments.rationale: Playwright MCP and Chrome DevTools MCP together covered route smoke, modal/detail flows, and accessibility/perimeter diagnostics in one broad run.

usageChecks.summary: vercel usage --format json --non-interactive reviewed during production broad QA; effective usage remained 15.0518 USD while billed amount remained 0.0000 USD.

artifacts.label: v8.11.28 broad AI workspace screenshot via Chrome DevTools

pendingImprovements.title: Landing profile button accessible name should satisfy label-in-name audit
pendingImprovements.note: Lighthouse flagged #profile-menu-button with label-content-name-mismatch on production v8.11.28; local fix is prepared but not yet deployed in this run.

notes: Transient /api/system net::ERR_ABORTED entries were observed during route transitions, but the same surface later completed /api/system 200 and no runtime console errors followed.
404 route emitted the expected document-level failed-resource entry rather than an application runtime error.
This run does not supersede the broad green reference because of the landing profile button accessibility finding.
